text_blocks.text: On June 4, 2014, the NFL announced that the
practice of branding Super Bowl games with Roman numerals, a
practice established at Super Bowl V, would be temporarily
suspended, and that the game would be named using Arabic
numerals as Super Bowl 50 as opposed to Super Bowl,
The use of Roman numerals will be reinstated for Super Bowl
LI. Jaime Weston, the league's vice president of brand and
creative, explained that a primary reason for the change was the
difficulty of designing an aesthetically pleasing logo with the
letter "L using the standardized logo template introduced a Super
Bowl XLV. The logo also deviates from the template by
featuring large numerals, colored in gold, behind the Vince
Lombardi Trophy, instead of underneath and in silver as in the
